forecasts.reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff of May 3, 2025, Muqtada al-Sadr is officially maintaining a boycott of the November 11, 2025, Iraqi parliamentary elections. He reaffirmed this stance as recently as late March 2025 and has ignored pleas from the Iraqi President and other political leaders to rejoin. However, there are strong tactical signs of a planned return: he rebranded his movement to the 'National Shia Movement' in 2024 and, critically, his associates instructed followers in late April 2025 to renew their voter registration cards. The Iraqi Independent High Electoral Commission (IHEC) set a party registration deadline for May 23, 2025. This deadline creates significant pressure for an announcement or registration before the July 1, 2025 resolution date. While Sadr has a history of strategic reversals, he also demonstrated a willingness to follow through on a boycott by sitting out the 2023 provincial elections. The 'voter card' signal suggests he is preparing for a return, but his current firm public commitment to a boycott and his tendency to wait until the absolute last moment (or even past deadlines via special extensions) makes an announcement before July 1 a moderate possibility rather than a certainty.
